description: Windows Remote Management (HTTP) — remote PowerShell sessions, command execution, and file transfer. Port 5986 is the HTTPS variant.

tools.name: evil-winrm
tools.description: Ultimate WinRM shell with file transfer, PowerShell script loading, .NET binary execution, and multiple auth methods.

commands.title: Login with Kerberos
commands.description: Connect using Kerberos authentication (requires valid ccache via KRB5CCNAME).

commands.title: Login with certificate (ADCS)
commands.description: Connect using a client certificate and private key (AD CS exploitation).

commands.title: Load PowerShell scripts
commands.description: Connect and load all .ps1 scripts from the specified directory (use menu to invoke).

commands.title: Load .NET binaries
commands.description: Connect and make .NET executables available for in-memory execution.

commands.title: Upload a file (in session)
commands.description: Upload a local file to the target (run inside evil-winrm session).

commands.title: Download a file (in session)
commands.description: Download a remote file to your local machine (run inside evil-winrm session).

commands.title: Execute inline PowerShell
commands.description: Execute PowerShell commands directly without an interactive session.

commands.title: Bypass AMSI (in session)
commands.description: Attempt to bypass AMSI (Antimalware Scan Interface) in the current session.

tools.name: NetExec (nxc) — WinRM
tools.description: WinRM credential validation and remote command execution via NetExec.

commands.title: Check WinRM access with password
commands.description: Test if credentials have WinRM access (Pwn3d! = admin).

commands.title: Execute CMD command
commands.description: Execute a CMD command via WinRM.

commands.title: Execute PowerShell command
commands.description: Execute a PowerShell command via WinRM.

commands.title: Execute with PtH
commands.description: Execute a command via WinRM using pass-the-hash.

commands.title: Password spray
commands.description: Spray a password against multiple users via WinRM.

commands.title: Check WinRM with Kerberos
commands.description: Test WinRM access using Kerberos authentication.
